lines: The atmospheric pressure is dropping fast. If those barometers aren't lying, we're in for a massive static storm over the western ridge.
Water reclamation at Noctis has been failing. Buy extra scrubbers here, or you'll be drinking your own recycled sweat within a week.
Avoid the direct path through Noctis Labyrinthus if you're hauling heavy cargo. The crevasse bridges are unstable after the last tremor.
CO2 sublimation rates are up this quarter, but don't take your mask off. The partial pressure will still kill you in under forty seconds.
A private scout rig went silent near Olympus. Some say they hit a lava tube collapse, others swear they ran out of spare hydrogen.
Solar activity is spiking. If you're heading downslope toward the plains, make sure your habitat's electromagnetic shielding is fully charged.
The hydroponic bay here is down to soy-paste rations again. If you've got real dried fruit or coffee, you can trade it for premium spare parts.
They say the automated seed-drones are finally planting lichen in the low basin, but the soil toxicity is still melting the root systems.
Watch the descent path. Glacial ice sheets under the dust layer are sublimating, creating invisible sinkholes that can swallow a rover whole.
The surveyor team returned from the eastern rift with empty sample crates and pale faces. They won't talk about what they found down there.
GPS signals are completely useless inside the canyon system. If you don't have inertial guidance backups, you'll be driving in circles.
There's a methane leak in Sector 4. One spark from a bad rover battery, and you'll vaporize your entire caravan.
The temperature plunges sixty degrees in the shadow of the canyon walls. Keep your heating elements running, or the fuel lines will freeze.
Found a stripped chassis from the '84 survey run. Leftovers were picked clean, but the hull plating was torn from the inside out.
Early morning fog in the ravines is getting thicker. It's mostly frozen CO2 and silica dust, so don't let it clog your intake filters.
